perturbation.original_text: The undersigned understands and agrees that as to ---------------- GSMS he is an "affiliate" under Rule 145(c) as defined in Rule 405 of the Rules and Regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended ("1933 Act"), and the undersigned anticipates that he will be such an "affiliate" at the time of the Merger.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned understands and agrees that as to GSMS he is an "affiliate" but the definition of "affiliate" will be determined at a later date by PSS legal counsel, and the undersigned anticipates that he will be considered an "affiliate" at the time of the Merger.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Rule 145(c) and Rule 405 as defining 'affiliate,' the contract introduces ambiguity. While the term 'affiliate' is still used, its specific meaning is deferred to PSS legal counsel. This contradicts the intent of providing a clear, legally defined term upfront, and creates uncertainty as the affiliate status is no longer objective but subject to interpretation by one party. The contract continues to use the term "affiliate" in other sections, creating an internal contradiction on how it should be interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions on Transfer. The undersigned understands and agrees that ------------------------ stop transfer instructions with respect to the shares of PSS Common Stock received by the undersigned pursuant to the Merger will be given to PSS's transfer agent and that there will be placed on the certificates for such shares, or shares issued in substitution thereof, a legend stating in substance: "The shares represented by this certificate were issued pursuant to a business combination which is accounted for as a "pooling of interests" and may not be sold, nor may the owner thereof reduce his risks relative thereto in any way, until such time as PSS, Inc. ("PSS") has published the financial results covering at least 30 days of combined operations after the effective date of the merger through which the business combination was effected. In addition, the shares represented by this certificate may not be sold, transferred or otherwise disposed of except or unless (1) covered by an effective registration statement under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, (2) in accordance with (i) Rule 145(d) (in the case of shares issued to an individual who is not an affiliate of PSS) or (ii) Rule 144 (in the --- case of shares issued to an individual who is an affiliate of PSS) of the Rules and Regulations of such Act, or (3) in accordance with a legal opinion satisfactory to counsel for PSS that such sale or transfer is otherwise exempt from the registration requirements of such Act." Such legend will also be placed on any certificate representing PSS securities issued subsequent to the original issuance of the PSS Common Stock pursuant to the Merger as a result of any transfer of such shares or any stock dividend, stock split, or other recapitalization as long as the PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned pursuant to the Merger has not been transferred in such manner to justify the removal of the legend therefrom. Upon the request of the undersigned, PSS shall cause the certificates representing the shares of PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned in connection with the Merger to be reissued free of any legend relating to restrictions on transfer by virtue of ASR 130 and 135 as soon as practicable after the requirements of ASR 130 and 135 have been met. In addition, if the provisions of Rules 144 and 145 are amended to eliminate restrictions applicable to the PSS Common Stock received by the undersigned pursuant to the Merger, or at the expiration of the restrictive period set forth in Rule 145(d), PSS, upon the request of the undersigned, will cause the certificates representing the shares of PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned in connection with the Merger to be reissued free of any legend relating to the restrictions set forth in Rules 144 and 145(d) upon receipt by PSS of an opinion of its counsel to the effect that such legend may be removed. - 2 -
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines 'affiliate' using Rule 145(c) and Rule 405. The changed text removes this definition and defers it to PSS legal counsel. The contradicted text then uses the term 'affiliate' in the context of Rule 144, implying a clear, pre-defined meaning. By making the definition of 'affiliate' ambiguous and subject to future determination, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text's assumption of a fixed definition, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding transfer restrictions.

perturbation.original_text: PSS agrees that it will publish such results as promptly as practicable following the Merger in the sole discretion of PSS, but in any event within 45 days after the end of the first fiscal quarter of PSS containing the required period of post-Merger combined operations and that it will notify the undersigned promptly following such publication.
perturbation.changed_text: PSS agrees that it will publish such results at its earliest convenience. PSS is not obligated to notify the undersigned following such publication.
perturbation.explanation: The original text commits PSS to publishing financial results within a defined timeframe and notifying the undersigned. The altered text makes the timeline vague ('at its earliest convenience') and removes the notification requirement. This introduces a contradiction because section 4 still relies on the undersigned being notified of this publication in order to trigger the removal of transfer restrictions. Without a definite timeline and notification, the 'Restrictions on Transfer' (Section 4) become unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions on Transfer. The undersigned understands and agrees that ------------------------ stop transfer instructions with respect to the shares of PSS Common Stock received by the undersigned pursuant to the Merger will be given to PSS's transfer agent and that there will be placed on the certificates for such shares, or shares issued in substitution thereof, a legend stating in substance: "The shares represented by this certificate were issued pursuant to a business combination which is accounted for as a "pooling of interests" and may not be sold, nor may the owner thereof reduce his risks relative thereto in any way, until such time as PSS, Inc. ("PSS") has published the financial results covering at least 30 days of combined operations after the effective date of the merger through which the business combination was effected. In addition, the shares represented by this certificate may not be sold, transferred or otherwise disposed of except or unless (1) covered by an effective registration statement under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, (2) in accordance with (i) Rule 145(d) (in the case of shares issued to an individual who is not an affiliate of PSS) or (ii) Rule 144 (in the --- case of shares issued to an individual who is an affiliate of PSS) of the Rules and Regulations of such Act, or (3) in accordance with a legal opinion satisfactory to counsel for PSS that such sale or transfer is otherwise exempt from the registration requirements of such Act." Such legend will also be placed on any certificate representing PSS securities issued subsequent to the original issuance of the PSS Common Stock pursuant to the Merger as a result of any transfer of such shares or any stock dividend, stock split, or other recapitalization as long as the PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned pursuant to the Merger has not been transferred in such manner to justify the removal of the legend therefrom. Upon the request of the undersigned, PSS shall cause the certificates representing the shares of PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned in connection with the Merger to be reissued free of any legend relating to restrictions on transfer by virtue of ASR 130 and 135 as soon as practicable after the requirements of ASR 130 and 135 have been met. In addition, if the provisions of Rules 144 and 145 are amended to eliminate restrictions applicable to the PSS Common Stock received by the undersigned pursuant to the Merger, or at the expiration of the restrictive period set forth in Rule 145(d), PSS, upon the request of the undersigned, will cause the certificates representing the shares of PSS Common Stock issued to the undersigned in connection with the Merger to be reissued free of any legend relating to the restrictions set forth in Rules 144 and 145(d) upon receipt by PSS of an opinion of its counsel to the effect that such legend may be removed. - 2 -
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction. The original text specifies a timeframe (within 45 days after the end of the first fiscal quarter) for publishing financial results and mandates notification to the undersigned. The changed text makes the timeline vague ('at its earliest convenience') and eliminates the notification requirement. Section 4 ('Restrictions on Transfer') explicitly states that transfer restrictions remain until PSS publishes financial results covering at least 30 days of combined operations. The original text provides the undersigned a trigger point to request the removal of the legend on the stock certificates. By removing the specific timeline and notification, the changed text creates a situation where the undersigned may not know when the publication has occurred, making the restrictions on transfer potentially indefinite and unenforceable, thus contradicting Section 4. The lack of notification directly impacts the undersigned's ability to act on the publication, which is the condition for removing transfer restrictions. Therefore, it introduces a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If PSS's counsel concludes that such proposed sale or transfer complies with the requirements of Rule 145(d), PSS shall cause such counsel to provide such opinions as may be necessary to PSS's Transfer Agent so that the undersigned may complete the proposed sale or transfer.
perturbation.changed_text: If PSS's counsel deems it appropriate, PSS may consider providing opinions to PSS's Transfer Agent regarding a proposed sale or transfer.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause creates an obligation for PSS to provide necessary legal opinions if its counsel concludes a sale complies with Rule 145(d). The altered text makes this action discretionary ('may consider'). This omission creates a contradiction because the contract still states that transfers can be done in accordance with Rule 145(d) (Section 4), but removes the clear mechanism by which the affiliate can obtain the necessary legal clearance to do so. This makes the possibility of transfer under Rule 145(d) uncertain and dependent on PSS's goodwill.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions on Transfer. The undersigned understands and agrees that ------------------------ stop transfer instructions with respect to the shares of PSS Common Stock received by the undersigned pursuant to the Merger will be given to PSS's transfer agent and that there will be placed on the certificates for such shares, or shares issued in substitution thereof, a legend stating in substance: "The shares represented by this certificate were issued pursuant to a business combination which is accounted for as a "pooling of interests" and may not be sold, nor may the owner thereof reduce his risks relative thereto in any way, until such time as PSS, Inc. perturbation.justification: The original text mandated PSS to provide legal opinions if its counsel deemed a sale compliant with Rule 145(d). The changed text makes providing such opinions discretionary ('may consider'). This creates a contradiction with the 'Restrictions on Transfer' clause, which states transfers can be done in accordance with Rule 145(d), but the changed text removes the clear mechanism for obtaining the necessary legal clearance, making transfers under Rule 145(d) uncertain and dependent on PSS's goodwill. This contradicts the original intent of allowing transfers under Rule 145(d) as stated in the 'Restrictions on Transfer' section.
